title: Blotterbook — improvement backlog (paste-ready prompts)
intro: From the 2026-06-25 audit. Each item is a self-contained prompt: scope, goal, constraints, "done when".
tags: Tags: P1 do-soon · P2 durable win · P3 polish. Send them one at a time (one surface/concern per turn).
note: Committed source of truth for the ACTIVE engineering backlog (open + guardrail items; replaces the retired .claude/backlog.md). Completed (Done) items are archived in data/backlog_archive.json to keep this file lean — the admin Backlog panel reads BOTH and merges them, so done items stay visible. The admin panel shows category/effort/status only; prompt + doneNote stay in the JSON.
commitConvention: Conventional-commit titles (enables CH12 automated versioning): when committing, and ESPECIALLY when titling a PR / squash-merge, lead the title with a conventional-commit type so the version bump can be derived automatically — `feat:` (new capability → minor), `fix:` (bug fix → patch), `chore:`/`refactor:`/`docs:`/`style:`/`test:`/`perf:` (→ patch), and append `!` (e.g. `feat!:`) or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer for a breaking change (→ major). Scope it when useful, e.g. `feat(staging): …`. Untyped/legacy titles default to a patch bump. Keep the existing Co-Authored-By and Claude-Session trailers on the commit body.
donePolicy: When an item's status is set to `done`, delete its `prompt` field and keep only the `doneNote` — the doneNote is the shipped record of what was actually done, so the original prompt is redundant and only bloats this file (the admin backlog panel never renders done-item prompts anyway). Keep `prompt` on `open` and `guardrail` items, since those are still the actionable spec. Applies retroactively: a done item should never carry a prompt. RECURRING ITEMS: an item with `recurring: true` (e.g. R1 repo audit, CH16 staging→prod promotion) is a perpetual driver — NEVER set its status to done, set completedDate, or strip its prompt; it stays open across every run and its prompt leads with a RECURRING/REPEATABLE marker. Each run ships its output as new items, not by closing the recurring item. ARCHIVE (post-split): completed items now live in `data/backlog_archive.json`. When you set an item to `done` and strip its prompt, MOVE the item object out of backlog.json into backlog_archive.json (the admin panel merges both). Recurring items never move — they stay open in backlog.json.

items.title: Keep the Store interface as the only persistence seam
items.prompt: Guardrail for future work: all reads/writes stay behind `Store` (`app/store.js`) so the planned cloud/ sync tier can drop in a `CloudStore` with the same async methods. Reject any change that calls `indexedDB` directly from app/render code.

items.title: Second full repo audit
items.prompt: RECURRING — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done, set completedDate, or strip this prompt. Each run is a fresh full audit (this is the Nth, not a one-shot); the findings ship as NEW backlog items, and R1 itself stays open for the next pass. Read-only audit (NO code edits) of the current repo: architecture, code quality, duplicated code, and security posture — accounting for everything shipped since the first audit (symbol sanitization, `_headers` CSP, `tokens.css` + nav/footer partials, the `app.js` → 7-script split and the subsequent A20 migration to native ES modules — `state.js` shared-state object, single `<script type=module src=main.js>` entry, no build/deps, screenshot downscaling). Give concrete, prioritized findings, and append any new actions to this backlog in the same format. Done when: a written, prioritized audit is delivered and new follow-ups are added to the backlog.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: per-day trade table on calendar/curve day-select (Svelte)
items.prompt: From the 3rd R1 staging parity audit (gates A33). In the vanilla app, selecting a day — by clicking a calendar cell OR a curve point — renders a read-only INTRADAY TRADE TABLE for that day (app/data.js renderDayTrades -> #j_trades): a header 'Trades · N · <net P&L>' plus a table of each trade's Time / Symbol / Side / P&L (filtered by the active filters; shows 'No trades on this day' when empty). It ships on every surface incl. demo. The Svelte staging app shows ONLY the JournalEditor (day note) on day-select — there is NO per-day trade list. Add it: a read-only per-day trades table in the Svelte day-select area (e.g. inside the cal panel's extra snippet alongside JournalEditor, or its own block), built from the filtered active trades for selectedDate, matching vanilla's header + columns. Pure presentation (A29). Done when: selecting a day shows its intraday trades (count + net + per-trade rows), updates with the active filters, and an e2e asserts the table renders for a traded day.
items.doneNote: Added DayTrades.svelte — a read-only intraday trade table (Time/Symbol/Side/P&L + 'N · net' header) rendered in the cal panel's extra snippet alongside JournalEditor on day-select. Built from App's active-filtered trades for selectedDate (dayTrades derived), shows 'No trades on this day (with the active filters)' when empty. e2e asserts the table + net on a traded day.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Svelte mobile/responsive — body overflow-x + @media breakpoints
items.prompt: From the 3rd R1 staging parity audit (gates A33). The Svelte staging app has ZERO @media breakpoints and its <body> lacks overflow-x:hidden, whereas vanilla app.css pins body{max-width:100%;overflow-x:hidden} ('no horizontal page scroll on mobile') and has breakpoints at 900/760/620/560/460px. On a ~360-414px phone several staging layouts overflow horizontally and scroll the page: the calendar 8-col grid (52px + 7x1fr, no min-width/scroll fallback — vanilla gives it min-width + a scroll container under 620px), the manage-data wide table, and possibly the filter bar + topbar button rows. (Cost-setup + overview cards use auto-fit/minmax and are fine.) Fix: (1) add overflow-x:hidden to the Svelte :global(body); (2) make the calendar usable on mobile (horizontal-scroll container with a min-width, or collapse the layout) — never let it scroll the whole page; (3) give the manage-data table a mobile treatment (verify the existing scroll container, or hide secondary columns under a breakpoint); (4) add @media rules so the topbar/filter bar wrap cleanly at ~360-620px. Done when: at 360px and 414px there is no horizontal PAGE scroll, the calendar + manage table are usable, and the topbar/filters wrap; verify with a mobile-viewport check.
items.doneNote: Pinned overflow-x:hidden + max-width:100% on :global(html) AND :global(body) so the viewport scroller can't scroll sideways (overflow on body alone wasn't propagating); the wide 8-col calendar now scrolls within its own panel under @media(max-width:620px) (.calendar overflow-x:auto + .calgrid min-width:520px); #sv-app padding tightened under 560px; manage-data table already scrolls in its wrap. e2e (390px viewport) asserts body overflowX hidden, window.scrollX stays 0, and the calendar is its own scroll container.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: manage-data Overview stats section (Svelte)
items.prompt: From the 3rd R1 staging parity audit. The vanilla data-manager modal opens with an 'Overview' stats grid (#dm_summary — app/datamanager.js ~70, partials/app-modal.html): Trades count, Date range, Day notes count, Tagged trades count, Local size (KB). The Svelte ManageData.svelte has no such section. Add an Overview stats grid at the top of the Svelte manage-data modal showing the same five stats (trade count, date range first->last, day-notes count, tagged-trades count = tradeMeta size, and an approximate local size). Pure presentation from the data already loaded. Done when: the manage-data modal shows the Overview stats, matching vanilla.
items.doneNote: Added the Overview stats grid to the Svelte manage-data modal (parity with vanilla #dm_summary): Trades, Date range, Day notes, Tagged trades, and approximate Local size (Blob of exportAll). e2e asserts the 5 stat cells.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: manage-data Saved-filters section (apply/rename/delete) (Svelte)
items.prompt: From the 3rd R1 staging parity audit. The vanilla data-manager modal has a 'Saved filters' section (#dm_filters — app/datamanager.js ~117-125) listing each saved view with Apply / RENAME / Delete. The Svelte app manages saved views only from the FilterBar (save/apply/delete) and has NO RENAME anywhere, and no saved-filters section in the manage-data modal. Add a Saved-filters section to the Svelte ManageData modal mirroring vanilla: list each saved view with Apply, Rename (prompt for a new name, persist via the Store meta in the {id,name,f} shape), and Delete; reuse App's saveView/applyView/deleteView and add a renameView. Done when: saved views can be applied/renamed/deleted from the manage-data modal and rename persists; e2e covers rename.
items.doneNote: Added a Saved-filters section to the Svelte manage-data modal with Apply / Rename / Delete per view (parity with vanilla #dm_filters). Introduced renameView in App (persists the {id,name,f} shape via Store meta) — rename previously didn't exist anywhere in the Svelte app. Apply closes the modal. e2e covers rename.

items.title: Phase 4 parity polish: title tooltips on Svelte interactive controls
items.prompt: From the 3rd R1 staging parity audit (cosmetic). Vanilla puts hover title tooltips on many interactive controls (panel chevron/grip, calendar nav/Latest, etc.); several Svelte equivalents have aria-label but no title (e.g. Panel.svelte .chev, CalendarMonth.svelte .today/.nav buttons). Add matching title attributes to the Svelte interactive controls for hover discoverability parity. Low priority, no behavior change. Done when: the key interactive controls carry titles matching vanilla.
items.doneNote: Added hover title tooltips to interactive controls that had only aria-label (Panel collapse chevron, calendar prev/next/Latest) for discoverability parity with vanilla.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Svelte modal a11y — focus trap (B9) + body scroll-lock (B36) + Escape-to-close
items.prompt: From the final R1 staging-app parity audit (gates the A33 cutover). The vanilla app gives every modal three behaviors via app/ui.js modalOpened()/modalClosed(): a Tab/Shift+Tab focus trap with initial focus move (B9, modalFocusables ~157-173), a ref-counted body scroll-lock (B36, lockScroll/unlockScroll ~139-151), and Escape-to-close. The Svelte modals are missing these: StatCardModal.svelte and ExportReport.svelte have NO Escape handler and NO focus trap; NONE of the three (StatCardModal/ExportReport/ManageData) lock body scroll. (Note: the Svelte modals DO use role=dialog + aria-modal=true + click-outside-to-close, which is correct, so aria-hidden-on-siblings is NOT needed.) Build a shared Svelte <Modal> wrapper (this also closes the A41-deferred Modal item): overlay + role=dialog/aria-modal, Escape-to-close, click-outside-to-close, initial focus + Tab focus trap, and a ref-counted body scroll-lock (matching ui.js semantics so stacked modals don't clobber each other). Refactor StatCardModal/ExportReport/ManageData onto it. Done when: all three modals trap focus, lock body scroll while open (released on close), and close on Escape; e2e asserts scroll-lock release + Escape close.
items.doneNote: Added a shared modal a11y action (app/staging-svelte/modal.js): Escape-to-close, Tab/Shift+Tab focus trap with initial focus (B9), focus restore on close, and a ref-counted body scroll-lock (B36). Applied via use:modal to StatCardModal/ExportReport/ManageData (ManageData's Escape still cancels an open per-trade editor first). e2e asserts the scroll-lock + Escape-close on the Net modal.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Svelte equity curve axes — gridlines, date/$ tick labels, end-of-line value labels
items.prompt: From the final R1 staging-app parity audit (gates the A33 cutover). The vanilla performance curve (app/render.js renderCurve) draws horizontal gridlines with y-axis $ tick labels (niceTicks/axMoney), vertical gridlines with x-axis date tick labels (MM/DD), end-of-line value labels + end dots per series, nice-tick domain framing, and axis titles. The Svelte EquityCurve.svelte renders only the area/line(s), note dots, cursor, zero line, selection line, and a single min/tip/max row beneath — NO gridlines, NO axis tick labels, NO end-of-line value labels. Port the vanilla axis furniture to the Svelte curve (reuse niceTicks/axMoney — move them to core.js if needed, A29): horizontal+vertical gridlines, x date ticks, y $ ticks, per-series end value labels + dots, nice-tick domain. Keep the existing interactivity (overlays/cursor/notedots/drawdown band). Done when: the Svelte curve shows gridlines + axis tick labels + end-of-line values matching vanilla; e2e asserts a y-axis tick label and an end value render.
items.doneNote: Rewrote EquityCurve onto a measured-width viewBox (bind:clientWidth, like vanilla) so SVG text isn't stretched, and added axis furniture: horizontal gridlines + y-axis $ tick labels, x-axis date labels, and per-series end-of-line value labels + dots, with nice-tick domain framing. niceTicks/axMoney moved to core.js (shared with render.js). All interactivity preserved (overlays/cursor/note-dots/drawdown band/keyboard). e2e asserts a y-tick label + an end label.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Svelte calendar per-day trade count + win% in each cell
items.prompt: From the final R1 staging-app parity audit (gates the A33 cutover). The vanilla calendar (app/render.js renderCalendar ~444-448) shows, inside each traded day cell: day number, P&L, AND a meta line '<trades> tr · <winRate>%'. The Svelte CalendarMonth.svelte day cell shows only the day number (+ note dot) and the compact P&L — the per-day trade count + win-rate % meta line is absent. Add the meta line to the Svelte day cell from m.days (rec.trades + rec.wins/rec.trades), matching the vanilla format + styling. Pure presentation (A29). Done when: traded day cells show 'N tr · X%'; e2e asserts it on a traded cell.
items.doneNote: Calendar day cells now show 'N tr · X%' (trade count + win rate) beneath the P&L, derived from m.days (wins/trades). e2e asserts a traded cell's .dmeta.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Svelte manage-data per-trade delete
items.prompt: From the final R1 staging-app parity audit. The vanilla data manager can delete a single trade (app/datamanager.js dmDeleteTrade ~346: Store.deleteTrade(id) + Store.deleteTradeMeta(id) so the trade's tags/notes/screenshots aren't orphaned — B40, with a confirm). The Svelte ManageData.svelte only offers per-trade Edit, day-note delete, and Erase-all — no per-trade delete. Add a per-trade Delete control to the Svelte trade table that calls store.deleteTrade(id) + store.deleteTradeMeta(id) behind a confirm, then reloadAll(). Guard under the demo no-write rule (see A33). Done when: a trade can be deleted from the Svelte table (with its meta), the dashboard recomputes, and e2e covers it.
items.doneNote: Added a per-trade Delete control to ManageData → store.deleteTrade(id) + deleteTradeMeta(id) behind a confirm (B40 — meta not orphaned), emits trade:deleted (now logged in the activity terminal), then reloadAll. e2e asserts the table row count drops by one.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Svelte stat-card modal drill-down fidelity
items.prompt: From the final R1 staging-app parity audit. The Svelte StatCardModal.svelte drill-downs are simplified vs vanilla app/widgets.js CARD_VIEWS + cm* helpers: (1) Win card lacks the proportional wins/losses/scratch split bar (cmSplit). (2) The 'PnL by weekday' chart omits the per-day trade count in the label (vanilla cmDow appends ' (n)') AND filters out weekends (Svelte keeps only i>=1..5; vanilla shows all active weekdays incl. Sun ETH). (3) The Profit-Factor 'By symbol' view is a net-PnL bar chart in Svelte but a full table (Symbol | Trades | PF | Net) in vanilla cmSymPf. (Lower priority: curve viewBox is smaller and the histogram uses 8 bins vs vanilla 14.) Bring the Svelte modals to content parity with vanilla (reuse pure helpers; A29). Done when: the win card shows the split bar, weekday labels include counts and all active weekdays, and the PF-by-symbol view shows the table; e2e asserts the split bar + by-symbol table.
items.doneNote: Stat-card modal fidelity: the Win card gains the proportional wins/losses/scratch split bar; the PnL-by-weekday chart now includes per-day trade counts and shows all active weekdays (incl. weekends); the Profit-Factor 'By symbol' view is now a Symbol|Trades|PF|Net table (cmSymPf parity). e2e asserts the split bar + the by-symbol table.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Svelte Advanced Statistics — Avg Hold Time row
items.prompt: From the final R1 staging-app parity audit. Vanilla Advanced Statistics (app/render.js renderAdv ~489-566) shows an 'Avg Hold Time' row (formatted duration · held/n) when trades carry holdMs (fills-based platforms — Ninja/MotiveWave/etc.; computed directly from m.trades[].holdMs, not from compute()). The Svelte AdvancedStats.svelte never computes or shows hold time, so fills-based users lose it. Add the conditional Avg Hold Time row to the Svelte panel: compute avg from m.trades.filter(t=>t.holdMs>0), format with the same fmtDur, show '· held/n', and only render when holds exist. (Demo/TradingView data has no holdMs so the row stays hidden there — verify with a fills fixture.) Done when: the row appears for fills-based data with the vanilla format and is hidden otherwise.
items.doneNote: AdvancedStats adds the conditional 'Avg hold time' row (fmtDur · held/n) computed from m.trades[].holdMs — shown only for fills-based exports that carry hold durations, hidden for close-event exports (TradingView/demo). fmtDur moved to core.js (shared with render.js).

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Svelte cost panel — $/RT column + unknown-fee guidance note
items.prompt: From the final R1 staging-app parity audit. Two small cost-panel omissions vs vanilla app/render.js renderCalc/commSymRows: (1) the per-symbol commission table is missing the '$/RT' (round-turn = 2× per-side rate) column — vanilla columns are Symbol | Trades | Cts | $/side | $/RT | Commission; Svelte CostPanel.svelte has Symbol | Trades | Contracts | Rate/side | Commission. report.js already computes commRows[].rt, so the on-screen table just needs the column. (2) When any symbol uses a fallback exchange fee (the '*' flag), vanilla shows a guidance note below the table (render.js ~619: 'No published exchange fee on file — priced with a fallback estimate. Add the symbol to data/exchange-fees.json …'); Svelte only shows the '*' with no guidance (the Assumptions caveat mentions the flag but not the how-to). Add the $/RT column and the conditional unknown-fee note. Done when: the cost table shows $/RT and the guidance note appears when a symbol is fee-estimated.
items.doneNote: Cost panel per-symbol table adds the $/RT (round-turn = 2x per-side) column and a conditional unknown-fee note ('Add the symbol to data/exchange-fees.json for an exact figure.') shown when any symbol is priced with a fallback exchange fee. e2e asserts the $/RT header.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Svelte misc — session-pill legend, savedFilters reload, journal demo message
items.prompt: From the final R1 staging-app parity audit — a bundle of small parity items. (1) Session-pill legend: vanilla's pill is a button that toggles a legend popup explaining Online/Offline/Degraded (partials/app-topbar.html + widgets.js initWidgets, aria-haspopup/aria-expanded); the Svelte pill (App.svelte) is a static span — add the click-to-open legend popup with aria sync. (2) savedFilters stale after data changes: App.svelte reloadAll() refreshes allTrades/journalDates/tradeMeta but NOT savedFilters, so a backup-restore won't refresh saved views until reload (vanilla datamanager reloadFromStore reloads them) — add savedFilters to reloadAll(). (3) Journal demo message: vanilla shows an explicit 'day-notes disabled in demo' message; the Svelte JournalEditor just disables the control — add the message under the demo no-write rule (see A33). Done when: the pill opens its legend, restore refreshes saved views, and the journal shows a demo message in demo mode.
items.doneNote: Session pill is now a button that opens a status-legend popup (Online/Offline/Degraded) with aria-haspopup/aria-expanded + outside-click close; App.reloadAll() now also refreshes savedFilters (a backup-restore updates saved views); JournalEditor shows a 'read-only in demo' note and disables its controls under PAGE_MODE==='demo'. e2e asserts the pill popup opens.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Avg Win/Loss headline card + its detail modal (Svelte)
items.prompt: From the R1 staging-app audit (final pre-cutover review). The vanilla dashboard shows FIVE headline cards — net, win, pf, wl (Avg Win/Loss / payoff ratio), dd (render.js renderCards ~49-51) — each with an F14 detail modal (widgets.js CARD_VIEWS.wl ~396). The Svelte Overview (app/staging-svelte/components/Overview.svelte) renders a richer 10-card set but DROPPED the 'wl' card, and StatCardModal's MODAL_KEYS only covers net/win/pf/dd. The avg-win/avg-loss/payoff data still appears in Advanced Statistics, so this is a missing headline card + drill-down, not lost data. Restore parity: add a 'wl' (Avg Win/Loss) card to the Svelte Overview (value m.wl, sub avgW/avgL) and a 'wl' branch to StatCardModal mirroring vanilla CARD_VIEWS.wl (avg win/loss, long vs short, payoff). Add it to MODAL_KEYS. Reuse the pure metrics verbatim (A29). Done when: the Svelte Overview shows the Avg Win/Loss card, clicking it opens the wl drill-down, and an e2e asserts it. Blocks/feeds A33 (prod-cutover parity).
items.doneNote: Added the Avg Win/Loss headline card to the Svelte Overview (value ratio(m.wl), sub avgW/avgL) and a 'wl' branch to StatCardModal — avg win/loss/ratio stats + win & loss distribution histograms (hist()), mirroring vanilla CARD_VIEWS.wl; added 'wl' to MODAL_KEYS. Pure metrics (m.wl/avgW/avgL/pnls) reused verbatim (A29). e2e asserts the card renders and its modal opens with the distribution.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: calendar Week column (ISO week + weekly P&L) (Svelte)
items.prompt: From the R1 staging-app audit (final pre-cutover review). The vanilla trading calendar renders a left 'Week' column per row — ISO week number + that week's P&L total + traded-day count (render.js renderCalendar ~417, ~455-459; uses isoWeek()). The Svelte CalendarMonth (app/staging-svelte/components/CalendarMonth.svelte) is a plain 7-column Sunday-first grid with no week column. Restore parity: add the leading Week cell to each calendar row (ISO week no. via the shared isoWeek helper, weekly P&L from the same per-day metrics, day count), matching the vanilla layout/classes (.wkcell/.wl/.wp/.wd). Pure presentation of compute()'s m.days (A29 — no recomputation). Done when: the Svelte calendar shows the Week column with week number + weekly P&L + day count, styled to match, and an e2e asserts a week cell renders. Blocks/feeds A33 (prod-cutover parity).
items.doneNote: Rebuilt CalendarMonth's grid as a 6-row Sunday-first sweep (buildWeeks) mirroring vanilla render.js: each row gets a leading .wkcell with ISO week no. (shared isoWeek, moved to core.js) + weekly P&L (.wkpnl pos/neg) + traded-day count. Grid is now 8-col (52px Week + 7 days); off-month days render as empty cells; rows past the month stop (w>0 && !monthHit). monthNet sums the weekly totals. Pure presentation of m.days (A29). e2e asserts a week cell renders its 'Wk N'.

items.title: R1 staging-app cleanup: export tile colors, shared helpers/dedup, stale comments
items.prompt: From the R1 staging-app audit (final pre-cutover review). A bundle of low-risk cleanups in the Svelte staging app (app/staging-svelte/**) — none block cutover, but they reduce drift before prod/demo migrate. (1) Export report headline tiles lose pos/neg color-coding except Max drawdown: vanilla export.js applies cls() per tile, but app/report.js reportHtmlDoc (~106) only marks 'Max drawdown' neg. Carry a per-row tone from buildReport (or apply cls to Net/Take-home/Gross) so the exported tiles color-code like vanilla. (2) Duplicated sessionOf (RTH 09:30-16:00 vs ETH) lives in both App.svelte (~159) and render.js (~674) — drift risk; hoist a single sessionOf to core.js and import in both. (3) Hoist small duplicated Svelte helpers: readImage (JournalEditor + ManageData), the blob-download helper (ExportReport + ManageData — also unify the revokeObjectURL timing), and the ratio()/num() formatters (Overview + AdvancedStats + StatCardModal) into a shared module (e.g. core.js for the pure formatters, a small svelte util for readImage/download); optionally extract a shared Modal overlay wrapper used by ExportReport/StatCardModal/ManageData (one place for the overlay a11y/scroll-lock pattern). (4) Stale comments now that A34-A38 shipped: App.svelte (~11) 'app/demo mounts land in A33' and CalendarMonth's 'come in later A27 slices' note. (5) Latent: ActivityTerminal subscribes to the event bus with no unsubscribe (core.js has no offEvent); harmless today (the component never remounts — keyed reorder moves the instance, collapse hides only the body) but add offEvent + cleanup if components may remount. NOTE: two audit candidates were checked and are NOT bugs — saveTradeMeta already rebuilds tags/shots into plain arrays so no $state proxy reaches IndexedDB (store.js ~217-219), and the `void [setup...]` effect is the repo's intended dependency-tracking idiom. Done when: tiles color-code on export, sessionOf is single-sourced, the duplicated helpers are hoisted, stale comments are corrected, and lint/tests/e2e stay green.

items.title: Guardrail: verify Stripe webhook signature before provisioning
items.prompt: Guardrail for the payments scaffold: never provision an account/entitlement in `functions/api/webhook.js` before verifying the Stripe signature — HMAC-SHA256 over the RAW request body with `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, constant-time compare, and a timestamp/replay window. The endpoint is an inert 501 stub today; reject any future change that wires up provisioning without signature verification first.

items.title: Promote Staging to Prod
items.prompt:
REPEATABLE — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done. Run to promote staging-only feature(s) onto prod + demo. Canonical procedure: the README section 'Promoting a feature from staging → prod' (the gate-layer model + 8-step checklist) — read it first; this prompt is the operational driver, the README is the source of truth.

SCOPE: promote the feature(s) named in the request. If none are named, inventory the full staging delta and promote all of it (staging is a proving ground — every feature is destined for prod + demo). Build the inventory by grepping: `STAGING_PAGE` and `if(!STAGING_PAGE)` / `if(STAGING_PAGE)` guards in the `app/*.js` ES modules (there is no longer a separate `app/staging.js` — it was renamed to `app/widgets.js` and loaded on every surface; staging-only logic now lives behind `STAGING_PAGE` guards inside the shared modules); `<!--IF mode=staging-->` blocks in `partials/*.html`; inline markup in `app/staging.html` that isn't yet in a partial; and the `STAGING_ONLY` set in `scripts/bump-version.mjs`. List each feature and its gate layers before editing.

PER FEATURE: (1) un-gate JS — delete the `STAGING_PAGE` runtime guard so it runs on every surface, or move staging-only logic into the relevant shared `app/*.js` module. (A20: the app is native ES modules loaded via a single `<script type=module src=main.js>` entry — if you add a NEW module file, `import` it from `app/main.js` rather than adding a `<script>` to `partials/app-scripts.html`.) (2) un-gate HTML — move inline staging markup into the right `partials/app-*.html`, or widen its `<!--IF mode=staging-->` to the modes it should reach (normally all three). (3) DEMO 1:1 — the feature MUST appear on demo, single-sourced from the SAME partial (never fork a separate demo copy — that is exactly what drifted before), with every TRADE-mutating control `disabled` in the `<!--IF mode=demo-->` path AND guarded by `DEMO_MODE`; read-only features (activity terminal, session pill) and UI-preference state (saved layouts) just mirror over. (4) if a path is no longer staging-exclusive, update the `STAGING_ONLY` set in `scripts/bump-version.mjs`.

THEN: run `node scripts/build-includes.mjs`; verify all three surfaces in a browser (prod: feature works and persists; demo: feature visible with trade-mutations disabled and nothing saved; staging: unchanged); run the gate — `node scripts/test-adapters.mjs`, `test-version.mjs`, `test-auth.mjs`, `test-flags.mjs`, `test-tax.mjs`, plus build-includes/build-manifest idempotency. Title the commit/PR `feat:` (or `fix:`) so CH12 bumps prod by the right level automatically — do NOT hand-edit `data/versions.json`; staging keeps its lead. Add a `data/changelog.json` (F13) entry for the new prod version describing what shipped. Commit + push (open a PR only if asked).

Each run done when: the in-scope staging features are live on prod + demo at 1:1 (demo trade-mutations disabled), all three surfaces verified, the gate is green, a changelog entry is added, and the change is committed/pushed. Leave this item OPEN (repeatable).

items.title: Discussion & planning: Trade Replay (simulated market snapshots)
items.prompt: Scope the effort + requirements for a Trade Replay feature that lets users step through simulated market snapshots in time around a trade to review it for analysis. Cover: what data is needed (do current platform exports carry intra-trade price/time, or only fills? what extra market data would replay require and from where?), storage/perf implications for a client-side static-hosted app, the UI surface, and a phased build estimate. Done when: a written feasibility/effort estimate exists with concrete next-step items and open questions.

items.title: Discussion & planning: module menu (spawn modules onto the dashboard)
items.prompt: Scope a Quantower-inspired 'module menu': a menu listing all of the app's modules where the user clicks an item to spawn that module onto their dashboard. Assess fit with the current panel/widget system (data-key panels, drag-reorder/collapse, layout templates via ws_save), what spawnable/removable modules require vs today's fixed panel set, how user-composed layouts persist, and a phased estimate. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements + next-step items exist.

items.title: Discussion & planning: realtime resizable + snapping modules
items.prompt: Scope realtime resizable modules with Quantower-style snapping (windows/modules freely resize and snap/dock together). Assess against the current drag-reorder/collapse layout system and the CSS layout it uses, what a resize+snap engine needs (library vs custom), mobile implications, layout persistence, and perf. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements + next-step items + open questions exist.

items.title: Discussion & planning: economic calendar data on the calendar module
items.prompt: Scope adding economic-event data (FOMC and other standard futures-relevant events) to the calendar module, user-togglable, with ~5 years of historical events backed in. Assess: data sourcing/licensing, how/where the dataset is stored and shipped (static JSON? payload size for 5 years?), how events render on the calendar without crowding the existing trade/journal data, the toggle UX, and ongoing updates/maintenance. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements + next-step items + open questions exist.

items.title: Discussion & planning: Stripe integration (donations now, subscriptions later)
items.prompt: Scope the effort + requirements to set up Stripe. NEAR-TERM goal: accept donations — the user clicks/interacts with the pricing module on the homepage to trigger a Stripe donation flow. Cover: Stripe primitives for one-off donations (Payment Links vs Checkout Session vs Payment Element), what server side is needed on Cloudflare Pages (a Pages Function for the Checkout session + webhook, secret/key handling — no keys in the static client), the homepage UX hook on the existing pricing element, and test vs live mode. ALSO lay down scaffolding for monthly SUBSCRIPTIONS (Products/Prices, customer + billing portal, webhooks) even though subscriptions are a later goal gated on remote data storage + user accounts — note the dependency and what can be stubbed now vs deferred. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements (donations MVP path, subscription scaffolding, server/secret needs, open questions) + concrete next-step backlog items exist.

items.title: Add a copyright + operator line to the site footer
items.prompt: From R7. The shared site footer (partials/footer.html) has no copyright line and never names the operating entity. Add a '© 2026 <name>' line (and, on legal.html, a 'Blotterbook is operated by <entity>' note). The exact entity display name is a pending owner decision (TBD as of R7 — 'Scaline Audio' per LICENSE); ship brand-only '© 2026 Blotterbook' now if undecided and swap in the entity once chosen. Rebuild includes so every page picks up the footer change. Done when: every page footer shows a copyright line and the operator is named once the entity is decided.

items.title: Pre-monetization legal pack: ToS, Privacy Policy, Donations/Refund policy
items.prompt: From R7 — prerequisite for R15 (Stripe). Before taking money, expand the legal content (legal.html / new sections or pages): (1) a real Terms of Service distinct from the current disclaimer bullets, with a governing-law/venue clause set to DELAWARE; (2) a standalone full Privacy Policy (today it's a summary) covering local-only storage, the coarse-region tax lookup, and any Stripe data once payments land; (3) a Donations/Refund policy stating the $25/$50 tiers are voluntary NON-REFUNDABLE donations with no goods/services exchanged; (4) name the operating entity / seller identity (BLOCKED on the owner's entity decision from R7). Keep the existing not-a-broker/not-advice disclaimers. Link the new docs from the footer and from the donation/checkout flow. Done when: ToS (Delaware), Privacy Policy, and the non-refundable Donations/Refund policy are published and linked, and the seller identity is shown.

items.title: Clear stale "(staging)" labels after the F14 promotion + dead degraded-state branch
items.prompt: From the R1 audit (cosmetic drift from the CH16 F14 promotion). F14's stat-card detail modal now ships on all surfaces, but three comment/labels still call it staging-only: app/widgets.js ~116 ('F14 (staging) — stat-card metric-detail modals'), app/app.css ~174 ('clickable headline cards (staging)'), and app/app.css ~388 ('stat-card detail modal (staging)'). Update them to 'all surfaces · CH16' to match render.js/widgets.js/partials, so a future reader doesn't think the feature is still gated. Also: setSession('degraded') (app/widgets.js ~29-33) handles a state no caller emits (self-labeled 'reserved/forward-looking') — either wire it or drop the dead branch. Done when: no stale '(staging)' label remains for F14 and the degraded branch is wired or removed. R1 FOLLOW-UP (additional stale post-CH16 '(staging)' labels found in this audit, clear these too): app/export.js ~186 still heads the export-report modal feature 'F3 (staging)' though export.js ~177 says it was promoted to all surfaces in CH16; and scripts/build-includes.mjs ~24-26 header comment still claims the activity terminal is staging-ALONE and NOT duplicated, but CH16 promoted it to all three shells (now identical in app/demo/staging.html).

items.title: (homepage) Add topic graphics to the feature-detail panels
items.prompt: From F7's feature explorer. The homepage Features section (index.html + assets/home.js) shows a clickable feature LIST that swaps a detail panel on selection; those detail panels are currently text-only. Add a topic graphic to each feature's detail panel that visually represents the feature — reuse/adapt the app's OWN module visuals where possible (e.g. a mini equity curve for the performance feature, a month grid for the calendar/journal, a cost waterfall for the cost/tax feature, a stat-card cluster for statistics). Keep it dependency-free (inline SVG, colours from tokens.css), responsive (the explorer stacks to one column at md/760), and CSP-clean (no inline scripts/handlers — wire via assets/home.js). Done when: each feature-detail panel includes a relevant graphic, the panels still swap on selection, and the section renders responsively with zero console/CSP errors.

items.title: (staging) Review: purpose of the 'Tagged trades' Overview stat + what tagging means
items.prompt: Staging-scoped discussion. Review the 'Tagged trades' stat in the Manage data → Overview section: where its value comes from today (the count of trades in the trademeta store carrying tags), what tagging a trade currently MEANS in the app (per-trade tags set in the trade editor, surfaced in the dashboard Tag filter), and what it SHOULD mean as a workflow (setups? mistakes? strategies? review state?). Decide whether the Overview stat earns its place, how it should be defined/labeled, and whether tagging needs a clearer purpose or UX. Any resulting UI change is staging-only (proving-ground) until promoted via CH16. Done when: a written decision on the stat's meaning + tagging's intended purpose exists, with concrete next-step items if changes are warranted.

items.title: (staging) Make the 'Erase all local data' button red (danger styling)
items.prompt: Staging-only — gate to the staging surface; do NOT change prod/demo. In the Manage data modal's Danger zone, restyle the 'Erase all local data' button with a red/danger treatment so the destructive action is visually distinct from the neutral controls. Keep the existing confirm step. NOTE: the source request trailed off mid-sentence ('…and when') — a second requirement was likely intended (possibly a hold-to-confirm, a typed confirmation, or a disabled state). Ship the red-styling part now and flag the missing clause for the owner to clarify before building more. Done when: on staging the Erase button reads as a danger action (red), prod/demo are unchanged, and the open question about the second clause is recorded.

items.title: (staging) Remove the Changelog button from the dashboard top bar
items.prompt: Staging-only — gate to the staging surface; do NOT change prod/demo. Remove the 'Changelog' button from the app dashboard top bar on staging (it links out to the marketing changelog and is noise inside the journal app). Edit the shared top-bar source (partials/app-topbar.html) behind a `<!--IF mode!=staging-->` / mode conditional so prod + demo keep the button until/unless this is promoted via CH16, then rebuild with `node scripts/build-includes.mjs`. Done when: the staging dashboard top bar no longer shows the Changelog button, prod/demo are unchanged, and the build is idempotent.

items.title: (staging) Break-even & Cost Budget always computes from All Time (ignore filters/scope)
items.prompt: Staging-only — gate to the staging surface; do NOT change prod/demo. The Break-even & Cost Budget panel currently recomputes against the active scope toggle + filter bar like the rest of the dashboard. On staging, make it ALWAYS calculate from All Time — independent of scope and filters — so it reads as a stable, account-level cost budget rather than a filtered slice. Source it from the unfiltered trade set (not applyFilters()/SCOPE) inside renderCalc/costModel behind STAGING_PAGE, and update the panel copy to state it's all-time. Leave every other panel filter-driven. Done when: on staging, changing scope/filters does not change the Break-even & Cost Budget figures; prod/demo keep the current filter-aware behavior; no console errors.

items.title: (staging) New module: Trade Blotter (full-width scrollable trades table)
items.prompt: Staging-only — gate to the staging surface; do NOT change prod/demo. Add a new dashboard module, 'Trade Blotter': a full-horizontal-width panel containing a scrollable list of ALL trades — essentially the Manage data → Trades table surfaced on the dashboard. Default dashboard position: directly BELOW the Trading Calendar module (and it must respect drag-reorder/collapse + workspace layout templates). Columns per row: Date/time, Symbol, Instrument name, Contract quantity, Price, Commission, Note, PnL. Rows are READ-ONLY except the Note field, which is inline-editable and persists via the shared annotation/Store path (annTextField / trademeta) — all other editing stays in the Manage data menu. Reuse the existing trade-row rendering + the shared commission helper (commSymRows/costModel) rather than recomputing. Add the markup to partials/app-dash.html behind a `<!--IF mode=staging-->` conditional, render gated by STAGING_PAGE, register its data-key in DEFAULT_DASH_ORDER (in app-dash order), then rebuild with build-includes. Confirm whether the blotter should respect the active filter set/scope like other panels (default: yes). Done when: staging shows a full-width Trade Blotter below the calendar with the eight columns, scrollable, the Note field editable inline and everything else read-only; prod/demo are unchanged; build idempotent and tests green.

items.title: (staging) Performance graph: stack the gross/net/take-home gradient fills
items.prompt: Staging-only — gate to the staging surface; do NOT change prod/demo. On the Performance graph each plot (Gross/Net/Take-home) has a gradient area fill under its line (green/blue/pink), but with multiple plots enabled only the GREEN fill is visible — the topmost fill paints opaquely over the others. On staging, fix the gradient compositing so the enabled fills STACK/blend and each remains legible (e.g. render the fills back-to-front by magnitude, and/or lower the fill opacity so overlaps show through) instead of green sitting solid on top. Keep the line overlays and the hover/click-to-select-date behavior intact. (The source note trailed off at 'when…'.) Done when: on staging, enabling two or three plots shows all their gradients legibly layered, not just green; prod/demo are unchanged; no console errors.

items.title: (staging) Design review: distribute Definitions & Caveats into the relevant panels
items.prompt: Staging-scoped design review. Evaluate REMOVING the standalone 'Definitions & Caveats' dashboard module and DISTRIBUTING its contents into the relevant panels/menus — mirroring how Break-even & Cost Budget now carries its own 'Assumptions & Caveats' subsection (F6/F10). Go through EACH item currently in Definitions & Caveats and recommend which module/menu it should relocate to (e.g. metric definitions → Advanced Statistics or the F14 stat-card detail modals; scope/filter wording → the scope toggle / filter bar; cost/tax model notes → Break-even; date/session/timezone assumptions → wherever they surface). Produce a per-item mapping table and a clear keep-vs-distribute recommendation; weigh discoverability (one place to read everything) vs context (caveats next to the number they qualify). Any implementation is staging-first (proving-ground) until promoted. Done when: a written per-item relocation mapping + recommendation exists, with spun-off implementation items if approved.

items.title: (infra) Cache the per-visitor /api/* calls to extend the Workers free-tier headroom
items.prompt: Cloudflare Pages Functions count against the Workers FREE plan's 100,000 requests/day (combined Workers + Functions). Today every visitor triggers ~1-3 Function calls — `/api/geo` (tax-state prefill on the app landing), `/api/config` (feature flags at app boot), and the homepage `/api/status` Live-pill ping — so the daily cap is the app's real traffic ceiling and a spike could exhaust it (static assets keep serving; the `/api/*` calls start erroring). Reduce these per-visitor invocations: edge-cache `/api/status` and `/api/config` with a short TTL (Cache API / `Cache-Control`) since both change rarely and `/api/config` already mirrors the client-side APP_FLAGS defaults so a briefly-stale read is harmless; cache `/api/geo` by coarse region. Keep admin POST writes uncached. Confirm each call already fails safe when it errors / goes over-limit (geo silently no-ops, config falls back to APP_FLAGS, the pill shows a neutral state). Done when: a typical app session makes materially fewer (ideally cache-served) `/api/*` invocations, all three still work, and each degrades gracefully under the 100k/day cap — with a before/after note on invocations per session.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Rate-data scraper must stay within the 50-subrequest free-tier cap
items.prompt: Guardrail for the roadmap's scheduled rate-data scraper (the Cron Worker that will pull CME fee schedules, per-broker data-feed prices, and per-state tax tables into `data/*.json`). The Workers FREE plan allows only 50 EXTERNAL subrequests per invocation. When this is built, design within that cap: batch/limit outbound fetches to <=50 per run (count the GitHub commit-back call), split sources across multiple scheduled invocations, or move to Workers Paid. Reject a single-invocation design that fans out to dozens of sources on the free tier.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Move to Workers Paid before shipping the paid cloud-sync tier
items.prompt: Guardrail for the planned cloud-sync subscription tier (the `CloudStore` behind the `Store` seam, A4). Every device push/pull is a Pages Function invocation against the Workers FREE plan's 100,000 requests/day cap — a few hundred active sync users would exhaust it — and KV's 1,000 writes/day free cap is far too low for user data. Do NOT ship a paying sync product on the free Workers plan: treat the Workers Paid plan ($5/mo — 10M requests + higher KV/CPU) as a prerequisite, with R2 (10 GB free, free egress — fits the zero-knowledge model) for encrypted trade blobs and D1/KV for accounts/entitlements. Plan the economics around Workers Paid (trivial against subscription revenue), not around stretching the free tier. Reject a sync rollout that assumes the free request/KV caps.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Keep Workers KV low-write — never store per-visitor state in KV
items.prompt: Guardrail for KV usage (`STATUS_KV`). The Workers FREE plan caps KV at 1,000 WRITES/day (plus 1,000 deletes + 1,000 lists; reads are 100k/day; 1 GB storage). Today KV holds only admin-set status + feature-flag config and the best-effort admin rate-limiter — all low-write and fail-open, which is fine. Keep it that way: never put per-VISITOR or per-REQUEST state in KV (counters, analytics, sessions, public-endpoint rate-limiting) — that would blow 1,000 writes/day almost immediately and start erroring. New per-user/per-request persistence belongs in D1/R2 (and on Workers Paid). Reject a change that writes to KV on a public or high-traffic path.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Repo layout is the deploy contract — no /src+bundler reorg
items.prompt: Guardrail from R16's structure review. Because the project has NO build step, the repo root IS the Cloudflare Pages deploy/web root — the folder layout is simultaneously the public URL structure AND the deploy contract. Do NOT adopt a '/src vs /public with a bundler' reorganization: it either changes every public URL (breaking canonicals/sitemap/_redirects/bookmarks) or requires a build/copy step that violates the 'no build step / deploys as static files' design pillar. Keep root = web root (functions/ MUST stay at root for Pages Functions; _headers/_redirects/robots.txt/sitemap.xml/favicon stay at root). IF a reorg is ever truly needed, the ONLY Pages-compatible move is a public/ output-dir split (browser-served files under public/, Pages 'build output directory' = public, functions/+scripts/+partials/+tooling stay at root) — and it must update the FULL coupled set in lockstep: the absolute /app/ /assets/ /data/ URL references, _redirects, _headers, robots.txt, sitemap.xml + page canonicals (CH5/CH6), build-includes.mjs (the ['.', 'app'] HTML scan), build-manifest.mjs (data/* paths), and bump-version.mjs (the prod-shipping path prefixes). Reject piecemeal moves that update only some of these.

items.title: Guardrail: admin-endpoint security must not depend on the fail-open rate limiter
items.prompt: From the R1 audit. rateLimited() (functions/_lib/http.js ~19-30) returns false (ALLOW) whenever STATUS_KV is unbound and is best-effort/non-atomic; it is the only throttle on /api/status, /api/config, and /api/admin-key (token issuance, 20/60s). This is the correct trade-off (don't lock the admin out; endpoints sit behind Access + constant-time token/key compare), so it is defense-in-depth only. Guardrail: never let an endpoint's security DEPEND on rateLimited() — auth (Access JWT + constant-time compare) is the real control; reject any future endpoint that relies on the limiter as primary protection, and keep STATUS_KV bound in prod so the throttle is actually active. Adjacent: A17 (KV low-write), S11 (webhook signature).

items.title: Adopt Tier B: shipped-output build — TRIGGER FIRED, superseded by ADR-001 (A26)
items.prompt: RESOLVED-AS-ACCEPTED, not deferred. The R19 trigger has fired: the commercial pivot + the complex-UI roadmap (R11 trade replay, R12 module menu, R13 resizable/snapping modules, F23 Trade Blotter) require a charting/layout library + a component model, which require a shipped-output build. The decision and phased plan now live in docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md, which supersedes the Tier B deferral in docs/build-step-decision.md. Tier B is being adopted as a Vite build + Svelte SPA on the /app/ surface (marketing pages stay static). This umbrella item is superseded by the spawned implementation/guardrail items: A26 (Vite infra + deploy-contract reversal), A27 (Svelte on staging), A28 (dependency policy), A29 (preserve the pure-logic core). Done when: A26 + A27 have landed and prod/demo migration (ADR Phase 4) is tracked; this item then closes referencing the ADR.

items.title: (guardrail) Dev tooling stays dev-only — SUPERSEDED IN PART by ADR-001/A28
items.prompt: PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md. This guardrail was written for the R19 Tier A world where the shipped app was zero-dep and the committed files were the deploy artifact (so devDeps could never touch shipped bytes, and SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL=1 was set on Pages). ADR-001 reverses that: Tier B (Vite build) is being adopted, so build deps now DO produce shipped bytes and Pages WILL install + build. The replaced clauses: 'shipped output stays dependency-free', the drift gate as proof-of-no-shipped-deps, and SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL — those no longer hold after A26 lands. WHAT STILL APPLIES until/after migration: keep node_modules + build output (playwright-report, test-results, the Vite dist/output dir) gitignored; pin every dep with a committed lockfile. The NEW dependency discipline lives in A28 (minimal/pinned/audited; supply chain as a security control; local-compute pillar gates every dep). Reference A28, not this item, going forward.

items.title: Vite build infrastructure + deploy-contract reversal (ADR-001 Phase 1)
items.prompt: ADR-001 Phase 1 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md). Stand up a Vite shipped-output build and execute the deploy-contract reversal — ALL AT ONCE (no users yet, so isolate the infra change from any logic rewrite; the app code stays VANILLA in this phase). Scope: (1) Add `vite` as a devDependency, pinned + lockfile. (2) Multi-page config that lists every HTML entry — the marketing pages (index/howto/roadmap/changelog/legal/admin) stay STATIC-rendered, plus app/{app,demo,staging}.html; do NOT pull marketing behind an SPA shell (SEO/instant-load). (3) Execute the public/ output-dir split from A18: browser-served files build → the Pages output dir; functions/, scripts/, partials/, and tooling stay at the repo ROOT. (4) Update the FULL A18 coupled-path map in lockstep — the absolute /app/ /assets/ /data/ refs, _redirects, _headers, robots.txt, sitemap.xml + page canonicals (CH5/CH6), and the path prefixes in build-includes.mjs / build-manifest.mjs / bump-version.mjs. (5) Cloudflare Pages: set build command (`npm run build`) + output directory (`dist`), and REMOVE SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL=1 (the build now needs devDeps at deploy — reverses the A25 posture). (6) CSP: the style-src 'unsafe-inline' drop (S18) is DEFERRED to A27 — the vanilla /app/ surface still has inline styles that the Svelte rewrite removes naturally, so doing it now is throwaway work; A26 leaves _headers unchanged. (7) Keep data/*.json static + runtime-fetched + cache-busted: RETAIN build-manifest.mjs (a rate change stays 'edit JSON, rebuild manifest, no app rebuild'); Vite hashes the APP assets. Re-confirm the HARD local-compute pillar (no telemetry/egress) survives. IMPLEMENTED (branch claude/vite-integration-eval): vite.config.mjs (9 HTML entries → dist/, publicDir disabled), scripts/copy-static.mjs (verbatim data/_headers/_redirects/robots/sitemap/og-image into dist/), npm run build/dev/preview, .node-version=22, playwright builds+serves dist/, CI adds a Build step. URLs preserved 1:1 so the A18 coupled-path refs needed NO edits. Source files NOT moved (build-manifest/bump-version/README untouched). Done when: all surfaces build via Vite + boot clean (e2e green — DONE locally), build-script idempotency holds (DONE), and the Pages dashboard settings above are applied + a deploy verified (PENDING — dashboard access required).

items.title: Adopt Svelte on the staging app surface (ADR-001 Phase 2)
items.prompt: ADR-001 Phase 2 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md) — depends on A26. Introduce Svelte and rewrite the STAGING app surface's VIEW LAYER as Svelte components; prod (app/app.html) + demo (app/demo.html) stay vanilla until staging proves out (mirrors the CH16 staging→prod proving-ground model). Scope: (1) Add `svelte` (v5, runes-first — use $state/$derived/$effect/$props, NOT legacy export let / reactive-$:) + `@sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte`, pinned + lockfile. (2) Mount a Svelte app into app/staging.html; rebuild the staging panels/widgets/modals as components. (3) Import the pure-logic core VERBATIM per A29 — app/adapters.js, compute(), costModel(), the Section-1256 tax model, app/store.js (Store), assets/util.js — do NOT rewrite them; the framework touches the view layer only. (4) Preserve staging's isolated-IndexedDB + key-gate behavior and the STAGING_PAGE-gated features. (5) CSP (S18) starts here: Svelte's scoped CSS + CSSOM remove the inline style attributes on staging as a side effect — begin retiring style-src 'unsafe-inline'. The GLOBAL flip to style-src 'self' needs every surface de-inlined (prod/demo in Phase 4 + moving the marketing pages' inline <style> to linked CSS), so the final _headers change lands when the last inline style is gone; S18 stays the tracking item. Done when: the Svelte staging app reaches feature parity with the vanilla app, e2e is green, the staging key-gate + isolated DB behave unchanged, and prod/demo are untouched. Follow-up (Phase 4, separate item when reached): migrate prod + demo to Svelte and retire the vanilla view layer, re-verifying the demo-never-persists invariant. STATUS (branch claude/vite-integration-eval): FOUNDATION LANDED — svelte@5.56.4 + plugin@7.1.2 pinned + svelte() in Vite; app/staging.html is now a Svelte mount (no partials markers; S14 token-strip + noindex/no-referrer preserved); app/staging-svelte/ (main.js + App.svelte + Overview/StatCard) boots via the verbatim core (loadRefData → isolated Store → seed → compute) and renders the Overview; demoCSV extracted to app/sampledata.js (re-exported by data.js) so staging doesn't bundle the vanilla view; toolchain wired (prettier ignores .svelte, tsc excludes the svelte dir). e2e green. CORE DASHBOARD COMPLETE on staging — all major panels are live in Svelte 5: Overview, performance equity curve, trading calendar, day-notes journal, advanced statistics, break-even/cost, filters+scope, manage-data modal + per-trade editor, and the activity terminal. Each reuses the pure-logic core verbatim (A29); the cost panel reuses costModel() as-is by rendering the DOM inputs it reads (the costModel(inputs) refactor stays deferred to Phase 4 per the owner's call). Full Playwright coverage. DEFERRED sub-features (polish, not blockers; during/after Phase 4): equity-curve overlays (gross/net/take-home) + hover tooltip + keyboard a11y + note dots; per-day tags/screenshots + per-trade screenshots; session/tag filters + saved-filter views; and the style-src 'unsafe-inline' CSP drop (S18). NEXT: ADR-001 Phase 4 — migrate prod + demo to Svelte and retire the vanilla view layer (then A30 source-tree reorg).

items.title: (guardrail) Dependency policy — minimal/pinned/audited; supply chain is a security control
items.prompt: Guardrail from ADR-001 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md), replacing the zero-runtime-dep stance (A25). Runtime/build dependencies are now ALLOWED, under discipline — because for a privacy product handling financial data, the supply chain IS a security control. (1) EARN-ITS-WEIGHT: add a dep only when hand-rolling it is materially worse; keep writing small utilities by hand (assets/util.js). Heavyweight enablers qualify (the framework, charting like lightweight-charts, layout/virtualization libs); date/utility micro-libs do not. (2) SUPPLY CHAIN: pin every version + commit the lockfile; run `npm audit` in CI; apply Subresource Integrity / strict CSP to shipped bundles; keep the dependency surface minimal; consider vendoring the few critical libs so a registry compromise can't silently reach users. (3) LOCAL-COMPUTE PILLAR GATES EVERY DEP (hard): reject any dep or dep feature that phones home, ships telemetry/analytics, or could exfiltrate trade data — no exceptions, regardless of convenience. Reject any dependency addition that fails the earn-its-weight bar or the local-compute gate.

items.title: (guardrail) Preserve the pure-logic core verbatim through the framework migration
items.prompt: Guardrail from ADR-001 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md). The Svelte migration (A27 + later Phase 4) must touch the VIEW LAYER ONLY. The pure-logic modules are the hard-won, test-covered value and must be imported into components VERBATIM, not rewritten: app/adapters.js (platform CSV adapters + detection + pairFills), compute() (metrics), costModel() (commissions/subscriptions/tax/take-home), the Section-1256 tax model, app/store.js (the Store seam — A4), and assets/util.js. They stay framework-agnostic so the node test suites (test-adapters/test-version/test-auth/test-flags/test-tax) keep covering them unchanged. Reject a framework PR that reimplements adapter parsing, the metrics/cost/tax math, or persistence inside a component instead of importing the existing module. The Store interface (A4) remains the only persistence seam.

items.title: Source-tree reorg (src/ + static split) — gated on the Phase 4 Svelte migration
items.prompt: DEFERRED — gated on ADR-001 Phase 4 (prod + demo Svelte migration), step 4d. The Vite build (A26) DECOUPLED the source layout from the public URL structure (Vite maps any source path to any output URL), so the A18 ban on a /src reorg NO LONGER APPLIES — a source-tree reorganization is now an optional cleanup, not forbidden. BENEFITS: (1) separate served SOURCE from non-served tooling (functions/scripts/partials/docs/configs at root today are mixed with site source); (2) resolve the mixed assets/ dir (bundled JS modules like home.js/util.js vs verbatim-static SVG/PNG) by splitting bundled vs a single static/public dir — which ALSO lets us adopt Vite's native publicDir and DELETE scripts/copy-static.mjs; (3) a Svelte-shaped structure (components/lib/routes). COST: it IS the A18 lockstep migration — moving source files changes the path PREFIXES that build-includes/build-manifest/bump-version/eslint/jsconfig/.prettierignore/README image refs key off, plus every relative import (./core.js, ../assets/util.js) — so it must be done ALL-AT-ONCE with all coupled paths + imports updated together (URLs stay 1:1 regardless, per A26). WHY PHASE 4, NOT NOW: doing it during the in-flight A27 buildout creates huge rename diffs that bury the real work; the final structure depends on the Svelte shape (don't reorg twice); and the flat layout's last advantage — the root tree still being directly servable un-built — is already eroding (staging.html can't be served un-built now) and disappears entirely once prod/demo are Svelte, so the cost/benefit only improves up to Phase 4. Settle the structure ONCE, after A33. Done when: either the reorg lands with Phase 4 (target layout decided; all coupled paths + imports updated in lockstep; URLs still 1:1; copy-static.mjs retired in favor of publicDir; all surfaces verified) OR it is explicitly re-affirmed as deferred.

items.title: Phase 4a: unify into one mode-aware Svelte app + in-memory DemoStore
items.prompt: ADR-001 Phase 4a (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md). FIRST step of the prod/demo migration. Today app/staging-svelte/ is staging-only; make it the SHARED Svelte app that adapts by PAGE_MODE (document.body.dataset.mode), the same way the vanilla app/*.js are shared across surfaces. (1) Rename app/staging-svelte/ → app/svelte/ (update the staging.html mount + Vite input). (2) Mode-aware boot: 'app' → IndexedDB Store (blotterbook DB) + restore last session (no seed); 'demo' → an in-memory DemoStore (see below) + seed + NO persistence; 'staging' → isolated blotterbookStaging DB + seed + key-gate (unchanged). (3) DEMO PERSISTENCE — the HARD invariant 'demo never persists': implement DemoStore as a drop-in object exposing the EXACT Store interface (A4 — addTrades/getAllTrades/saveJournal/getJournal/journalDates/saveTradeMeta/allTradeMeta/getMeta/setMeta/exportAll/importAll/purge/tradeId/validShot/local) backed by in-memory Maps, selected at boot by mode so the view code is unchanged. Trade-mutating controls are ALSO disabled/hidden in demo. (4) Do NOT switch app.html/demo.html to Svelte yet (that's A33) — this step only generalizes the app + adds DemoStore, still mounted on staging. Done when: the staging Svelte app runs through the mode-aware boot, a DemoStore exists + is unit-or-e2e-verified to never touch IndexedDB, and staging behavior is unchanged; tests green.

items.title: Phase 4b: finish prod-parity features on the Svelte app (absorbs A27 deferred items)
items.prompt: ADR-001 Phase 4b (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md). Bring the Svelte app to FULL parity with the vanilla prod app BEFORE cutting prod/demo over (A33) — prod must not regress below today's features. These are the A27-deferred items, now required: (1) Equity curve — gross/net/take-home overlays (toggle), hover tooltip (aria-live), keyboard a11y, day note-dots, and the curve↔calendar day cross-link. (2) Journal — per-day tags + screenshots; per-trade screenshots in the editor (route ALL screenshots through Store.validShot's base64-image allow-list, S15/S18). (3) Filters — session filter + tag filter + saved-filter views (persisted via Store meta). (4) Landing/setup/CSV-load gate flow for 'app' mode (broker/feed/state required before Start; staging seeds instead). (5) costModel(inputs) refactor: replace the A27 DOM-input reuse with a clean param signature in core.js (behavior-preserving — default to the current DOM reads so any remaining vanilla caller is unchanged), then remove the DOM controls CostPanel.svelte renders only to feed costModel. Keep reusing the pure-logic core verbatim otherwise (A29). Done when: every vanilla prod dashboard feature has a Svelte equivalent, costModel takes inputs, and the staging Svelte app is at parity; tests + e2e green. STATUS (merged to main): CORE COMPLETE, NOT FULL PARITY. Shipped as sub-slices: costModel(inputs) refactor (CostPanel drops the DOM-id hack); curve interactivity (note-dots, cursor+aria-live tooltip, click-to-select); session/day-of-week/tag filters; saved-filter views; curve gross/net/take-home overlays (extracted pure app/curveseries.js shared with vanilla render.js; cost setup lifted to App); journal per-day tags+screenshots + per-trade screenshots (also fixed a Svelte $state-proxy → IndexedDB clone bug + closed a saveTradeMeta validShot gap); app-mode landing/CSV-load flow. CORRECTION: a post-merge feature-by-feature audit found parity is only ~65-70% — 'complete' was over-claimed. The remaining prod/demo features (export report, stat-card detail modals, panel collapse/drag/workspace system, Definitions & Caveats, plus Tier-2 items) are now tracked as A34-A38 and MUST land before A33 so prod doesn't regress. A33 stays blocked until A34-A38 close.

items.title: Phase 4c: cut prod + demo over to Svelte, delete the vanilla view layer, finish S18
items.prompt: ADR-001 Phase 4c (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md). FINAL prod/demo cutover — do AFTER A31 + A32 AND the parity gaps A34-A38 (a post-merge audit found A32 only reached ~65-70% parity; cutting over before A34-A38 close would regress prod). (1) Convert app/app.html + app/demo.html into hand-authored Svelte mounts of the shared app/svelte/ app (like staging.html), each with its data-mode; remove the partials/app-*.html include markers from them. (2) DELETE the vanilla view layer once nothing references it: app/render.js, app/ui.js, app/datamanager.js, app/widgets.js, app/export.js, app/main.js, and the view-only parts of app/data.js (keep feature-flag defaults if still used by the Svelte app). KEEP the pure-logic core: app/adapters.js, compute/costModel in app/core.js, app/store.js, app/sampledata.js, assets/util.js, app/types.js — and the node test suites covering them. (3) Update scripts/build-includes.mjs (drop the app-shell assembly; keep nav/footer for the info pages) and scripts/bump-version.mjs (app surfaces are now Svelte mounts). Remove the now-dead partials/app-*.html. (4) Finish S18: Svelte scoped CSS removes the app inline styles; move the marketing pages' inline <style> (index.html, admin.html) into linked CSS; then flip _headers CSP to style-src 'self' (drop 'unsafe-inline'). (5) VERIFY all surfaces — app: landing→load CSV→Start→dashboard→reload restores from IndexedDB; demo: explore + ASSERT nothing written to IndexedDB + write controls disabled (the demo-never-persists invariant); staging: unchanged. Done when: all three surfaces are Svelte, the vanilla view modules + app partials are deleted, build-includes/bump-version updated, style-src is 'self' with no CSP violations, and the full e2e suite (incl. the demo no-persist check) is green. Then A30 (source-tree reorg) can follow.

items.doneNote: Built app/report.js — a pure buildReport(m,c,labels) + reportHtmlDoc(rep,labels,tokenBlock) shared by the on-screen iframe report, the Markdown download, and the email summary (A29; single source so they can't drift). ExportReport.svelte renders the standalone doc in an isolated, baked-token iframe (A8) and supports PDF (print iframe), PNG/JPEG (rasterize), Markdown, and Email — full parity with vanilla export.js. Wired the topbar Export-report button (shown when loaded && trades present) with broker/feed/state/scope labels from setup. e2e covers opening the modal, the iframe preview, format→Download enable, and the mailto link.
items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: export / performance report (Svelte)
items.prompt: ADR-001 Phase 4 parity gap (blocks A33). The vanilla app/export.js performance report (the top-bar Export button → print→PDF / image / markdown download + email-a-copy of a condensed summary) has NO Svelte equivalent. Build it for the Svelte app (app/svelte/): an Export control that produces the same condensed performance report from the active metrics + costModel. Reuse the report-DATA assembly logic verbatim where it's pure (A29); only the view/print layer is rewritten in Svelte. Cover the same outputs the vanilla report did (at minimum print→PDF; match the markdown/email summary). Done when: the Svelte app can generate the performance report on all surfaces it ships to, parity with export.js, e2e covers the report opening.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: clickable stat-card detail modals (F14)
items.prompt: ADR-001 Phase 4 parity gap (blocks A33). In vanilla (widgets.js openCardModal / CARD_VIEWS), the headline Overview cards are CLICKABLE and open detail drill-down modals: Net PnL (cumulative curve + cost waterfall gross→commissions→subscriptions→net→tax→take-home), Win Rate (win/loss/scratch + PnL-by-weekday + long vs short), Profit Factor (gross profit vs loss + per-symbol profitability), Avg Win/Loss (win/loss histograms), Max Drawdown (equity curve with peak→trough markers). The Svelte Overview cards are static. Make them clickable and build the detail modals as Svelte components, deriving everything from the compute()/costModel() metrics (A29 — reuse the dowBuckets etc. helpers from core). Done when: the relevant Overview cards open faithful detail modals, parity with F14; e2e covers opening a card modal.

items.doneNote: Built a Svelte panel framework: Panel.svelte provides unified chrome (drag grip, title, actions snippet, collapse chevron with aria-expanded/label — B41) and a collapsible body; the six dashboard sections (perf/cal/cost/adv/defs/term) each render their own <Panel> and forward a per-key prop bundle from App. App owns panelOrder + collapsedPanels + the draggingKey, persisted through the Store.local seam under staging-namespaced keys (tj_order_staging / tj_collapsed_staging), with whole-panel drag-to-reorder (grip arms draggable; dragover reorders by pointer midpoint). WorkspaceBar.svelte + App save/load/revert named workspace templates (tj_ws_templates_staging); '— Default —' restores DEFAULT_ORDER. Save is guarded off in demo (B23). The day-note editor rides inside the cal panel via a snippet so it moves/collapses with it. ActivityTerminal logs ws:saved/loaded/reverted. e2e covers collapse + reload persistence + template save/revert/reload. Parity with vanilla ui.js/widgets.js (drag simulation itself not asserted — HTML5 DnD is unreliable under Playwright).
items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: panel system (collapse + drag-reorder + workspace templates + layout persistence)
items.prompt: ADR-001 Phase 4 parity gap (blocks A33). The vanilla dashboard panels are user-customizable (app/ui.js + app/widgets.js): collapsible panels (chevron toggle + aria-expanded — B41), drag-to-reorder by grip handle, workspace/layout TEMPLATES (save/load/reset to DEFAULT_DASH_ORDER), all persisted to localStorage (tj_order / tj_collapsed / tj_ws_templates, _staging-suffixed on staging) via the Store.local seam. The Svelte app renders a FIXED layout with none of this. Build a Svelte panel framework: a reusable Panel wrapper (title + collapse chevron with aria-expanded, drag handle) that the dashboard sections use; persist order + collapsed state + named workspace templates via Store.local (per-mode keys); a top-bar workspace dropdown + save/reset. Done when: panels collapse, reorder by drag, and layouts persist + restore across reload (per surface); workspace templates save/load/reset; parity with ui.js/widgets.js; e2e covers collapse + persistence.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Definitions & Caveats panel
items.prompt: ADR-001 Phase 4 parity gap (blocks A33). The vanilla dashboard has a Definitions & Caveats panel (partials/app-dash.html data-key=defs): a glossary (Trade, Win/Loss/Scratch, Net PnL, performance graph, broker & costs, scope toggle, …) + a Warnings section (max drawdown is realized-only, weekday/streak sample sizes can be thin, commissions are modeled estimates, calendar-day grouping, US dates & Eastern time assumed). No Svelte equivalent. Port it as a Svelte panel with the same content (single-sourced text). Done when: the Svelte dashboard shows Definitions & Caveats at parity; e2e smoke that it renders.

items.title: Phase 4 parity gap: Tier-2 bundle (session pill, calendar/cost/manage-data/landing/filter polish)
items.prompt: ADR-001 Phase 4 parity gap (Tier-2; should land before A33). Smaller vanilla features missing from the Svelte app: (1) session status pill (online/offline) — widgets.js setSession; (2) Trading Calendar week-summary column + jump-to-today button; (3) Cost panel 'Assumptions & Caveats' subsection (round-turn commission, per-symbol, subscription accrual, tax blend, break-even formula); (4) Manage-data DAY-NOTES list (the notes table, distinct from the per-trade editor — view/open/delete a day's note); (5) Landing platform-override dropdown (manual platform pick when Adapters.detect is wrong — Adapters.parse(text, platformId)); (6) filter-bar live trade-count (matching trades); (7) top-bar Changelog + Contact links; (8) performance-curve drawdown band when gross is the sole overlay. Port each at parity. Done when: each Tier-2 item has a Svelte equivalent; e2e covers the higher-value ones (session pill, calendar jump-to-today, day-notes list).

howToDrive: One item per prompt; one surface per prompt where possible.
Lead with the *goal*, not the mechanism; let me pick the workable path.
If you batch, name the 1–3 must-haves in case I run low on budget.
Keep using the change-plan + memory pattern for any multi-step item.
